title: 5 Interac e-TransferÂ® Settlement and Reconciliation
body_text:
5	Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
Settlement and Reconciliation
5.1	Interac
e-Transfer Settlement
Financial Institution Settlement
All completed Interac
e-Transfer transactions are posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account with separate totals for debits and credits. Settlement is in Canadian dollars and is posted on the following business day on the financial institutionâs statement as code ET. Settlement occurs on business days only.
The deadline for Interac e-Transfer transactions is 20:59:59 PT. Interac e-Transfer transactions sent after the deadline or on a non-business day are settled on the next business day.
Interac e-Transfers fees are posted separately from the transactions. See Section 5.5, Interac e-Transfer Fees.
Customer Settlement
When a customer sends, fulfills, or deposits an Interac e-Transfer transaction, or when an Interac e-Transfer transaction is directly deposited into the customerâs account, the debit or credit is immediately posted to the customerâs account. While the funds are available immediately to customers, settlement to your financial institutionâs internal Interac
e-Transfer GL doesnât occur until the EMTC or EMFC file is processed during reconciliation. See Section 5.2, Interac
e-Transfer Reconciliation Files, for more information. In some cases, failed Interac e-Transfer transactions may require a manual adjustment to the customerâs account.
Settling Interac e-Transfer Transactions for Financial Institutions outside of BC and Ontario
Some financial institutions outside of BC and Ontario donât settle directly with Central 1 for Interac e-Transfer transactions. Central 1 provides settlement files for non-BC/ON credit unions to their provincial centrals. These financial institutions settle with their respective provincial central.
5.2	Interac e-Transfer Reconciliation Files
Financial institutions can use two files to reconcile Interac
e-Transfer transactions against their banking system records and reports:
EMTC (e-Transfer Detailed Transaction File)
This file contains:
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that are currently outstanding (requested/pending/fulfilled)
Interac
e-Transfer transactions cancelled by the sender since the last settlement (cancelled)
Interac
e-Transfer transactions cancelled by the system since the last settlement (cancelled by non-customer)
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that have been completed since the last settlement (completed/deposited)
EMFC (e-Transfer Detailed Transaction File with Failed Transactions)
The EMFC is an optional file that contains the same details as the EMTC and includes the details of all failed Interac
e-Transfer transactions. Changes may be required to your financial institutionâs banking system to use the EMFC file.
To obtain the Detailed Transaction Files, go to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange and download the EMTCmmdd.DAT or EMFCmmdd.DAT file from your root directory. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for procedures on accessing the File and Report Exchange. The files are in text format, generated Tuesday through Saturday, and include transactions from 21:00:00 PT the previous day until 20:59:59 PT the current day. Files are provided for each calendar day. If there are no transactions on a given day, the files will contain only A and Z records (header/footer).
See the EMTC/EMFC File Specifications, Form 6195, for more information.
Financial institutions outside of BC and Ontario should consult their respective provincial central regarding the location and availability of the detailed transaction files.
Related Information
EMTC/EMFC File Specifications,
Form 6195
5.3	Reconciliation of Interac e-Transfer Transactions
Your financial institution is responsible for balancing:
Your banking system to the
EMTC or EMFC file
Its internal GL to the settlement posted to the financial institutionâs account held at its provincial central
Itâs recommended that the financial institution create an internal general ledger (GL) account for Interac e-Transfer settlement. The GL accounts are balanced against the EMTC or EMFC files, or the EMCD report. Depending on whether the customer sends or receives funds, the financial institution will debit or credit the customerâs account and post the offsetting debit or credit to the appropriate GL account.
The following table outlines how to proceed with different scenarios when importing the EMTC or EMFC file to the banking system.
Scenario
Action
The EMTC or EMFC file matches the records in the financial institutionâs banking system.
No action is required.
The EMTC or EMFC file includes the transaction, but the banking system does not.
Debit/credit the customerâs account.
The financial institution has the transaction, but it isnât in the EMTC or EMFC file.
Wait one day in case the transaction missed the deadline. If the incomplete transaction isnât found after one day, reverse the transaction for the customer.
The transaction is in the EMTC or EMFC file and in the banking system, but they donât match.
Investigate both transactions.
Matching Criteria
Match the following fields for each transaction:
Date
Reference
Amount
Account Number
Transaction Type
Dr/Cr
Status
Financial institutions in BC or Ontario can consult EMCU, EMCD, and ECCX reports for reconciliation purposes.
5.4	Adjustments for Interac e-Transfer Transactions
When an Interac
e-Transfer transaction or Interac
e-Transfer transaction cancellation fails, you may need to adjust the customerâs account. Failed Interac
e-Transfer transactions that may require a manual adjustment are displayed on the ECCX report with an exclamation mark (!) beside them. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Interac
e-Transfer transaction failure entries on the ECCX report that donât have an exclamation mark (!) beside them generally donât impact settlement. These entries are usually the result of Interac e-Transfer transactions that fail before the financial institutionâs banking host debits or credits the customer.
Figure 1: Transactions on the ECCX report that may require a manual adjustment
Common situations in which a failed Interac e-Transfer may require a manual adjustment include:
Interac e-Transfer Message Missing at Interac
The customerâs account was debited but Interac didnât receive the request to create an Interac e-Transfer Send Money transaction.
Tip
: 	The money doesnât leave the financial institution until the Interac e-Transfer transaction is completed by the receiving party. In cases where a customer is debited by the banking system for an Interac e-Transfer that fails before the request reaches
Interac, the funds will be in the financial institutionâs holding GL for Interac e-Transfer transactions.
Reversal Failed
A reversal message was sent to the banking host, but it wasnât posted to the customerâs account. The transaction will appear on the ECCX report.
In the following situations, an Interac e-Transfer transaction reversal is automatic, and a manual adjustment isnât required:
Sender Cancels an Interac e-Transfer Transaction
If the sender cancels the Interac e-Transfer transaction, the funds are automatically credited back to the senderâs account. The transaction appears on the EMCD report as âCancelled by customerâ.
Interac e-Transfer Transactions Refused by the Receiver
Interac e-Transfer transactions may be refused by the receiver. If this occurs, the sender will receive an email alert and must then cancel the transaction. If the transaction isnât cancelled, it will remain pending until the Lazy Sender process occurs.
Some transactions that appear on the ECCX report with an exclamation mark (!) beside them require further investigation to see if a manual adjustment is required. Proceed as follows to investigate items that are marked with an exclamation mark (!) on the ECCX report:
Check the CR, DR, and RDR response codes associated with the transaction. See Chapter 9, Interac e-Transfer Response Codes, for more information.
If the transfer is on the EMCD report as âcompletedâ, but the transaction wasnât posted to the customerâs account, post the transaction manually.
If the issue still isnât resolved, submit a trace request. See Chapter 7, Tracing Interac e-Transfer Transactions.
5.5	Interac e-Transfer Fees
When your customer sends or receives an Interac e-Transfer Send Money or Request Money transaction, Central 1 applies charges to your financial institution. Charges arenât reversed for cancelled transactions. The charges may include different components including Central 1 processing, EFM, and Interacâs pass-through fees. The amounts vary depending on the type of transaction and if the sending customer is registered at Interac as a retail or business customer. Interac also charges other ancillary fees for Request Money initiation, SMS / phone notifications, and high-volume recipients. Central 1 passes through these ancillary fees. These fees are charged to your financial institution monthly. Contact your Central 1 Senior Account Executive or your provincial central credit union for billing inquiries.
Interac e-Transfer fees are applicable regardless of the channel (e.g., API, digital banking platform) or integration approach used. Fees are charged monthly and are netted and settled as one total.
Note: âOn-usâ refers to transactions where both the sending and receiving financial institutions connect to Interac via Central 1.
Service
Fee
Description
Send Interac e-Transfer Transaction*
Per Transaction
A fee applies for each transaction sent, including Send Money, Autodeposit, account number routed, real-time and non-real-time transactions. This includes Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) and Interac fees. The fee applies to each transaction sent regardless of its final status (e.g., completed, pending, cancelled).
Send Interac e-Transfer On-us Transaction*
Per Transaction
A fee applies for each transaction sent, including Send Money, Autodeposit, account number routed, real-time and non-real-time transactions. This includes Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) and Interac fees. The fee applies to each transaction sent regardless of its final status (e.g., completed, pending, cancelled).
Receive Interac e-Transfer Transaction
Per Transaction
The applicable Central 1 fee (EFM is included within this fee) for each transaction received.
Retail Request Money Initiation
Per Request
The Interac fee for each retail Request Money transaction. This fee applies to each Request Money transaction that was initiated regardless of its final status (e.g., completed, pending, cancelled).
Retail Request Money Transaction*
Per Transaction
A fee applies for each retail Request Money transaction that is fulfilled by the responder or recipient. The fee applies to the financial institution that sent the request. Includes Central 1 fee (EFM is included within this fee) and Interac transaction fees.
Retail Request Money On-us Transaction*
Per Transaction
A fee applies for each retail Request Money transaction that is fulfilled by the responder or recipient where both the requestor and responderâs financial institution connect to Interac via Central 1. The fee applies to the financial institution that sent the request. This includes Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) and Interac transaction fees.
Retail Request Money Responderâs Transaction
Per Transaction
Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) applies for each retail Request Money transaction fulfilled by the responder or recipient. This fee applies to the financial institution that received or responded to the request.
Retail Request Money Responderâs Reimbursement
Per Transaction
A reimbursement applies from Interac to the responderâs financial institution for each retail Request Money transaction fulfilled by the responder or recipient. This amount will be a credit.
Business Request Money â
Ad Valorem
Transaction
$ Amount
Calculated by Interac
The Central 1 fee (EFM is included within this fee) for each Request Money transaction sent from a business or commercial account that is fulfilled by the responder or recipient. This fee applies to the financial institution that sent the request and is only charged if the receiving or responding financial institution fulfills the request.
Business Request Money
â Ad Valorem
Amount*
Per Transaction
This is the total consolidated amount of the
Ad Valorem
fees that Interac calculates for the previous month. For each business Request Money transaction, the Interac fee is 35 basis points up to a maximum of $3.50 per transaction. Interac caps the
Ad Valorem
fee at a transaction value equal to $1,000. For example, the Ad Valorem fee on a $500 transaction is $1.75. ($500 x 0.35%).
Business Request Money Responderâs â
Ad Valorem
Transaction
Per Transaction
Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) applies for each business Request Money transaction fulfilled by the responder or recipient. This fee applies to the financial institution that received or responded to the request.
Business Request Money Responderâs Reimbursement â
Ad Valorem
Amount*
$ Amount
Calculated by Interac
This fee is the total consolidated amount of the
Ad Valorem
reimbursement that Interac calculates for the previous month. For each Business Request Money transaction fulfilled by the responder or recipient, the Interac reimbursement is 25 basis points up to a maximum of $2.50 per transaction. This amount represents the total from Interac and will be a credit.
Business Request Money Responderâs â Flat Fee Transaction
Per Transaction
Central 1âs fee (EFM is included within this fee) applies for each Business Request Money transaction fulfilled by the responder or recipient. This fee applies to the financial institution that received or responded to the request.
Business Request Money Responderâs Reimbursement â Flat Fee*
Per Transaction
If the Requestor meets Interacâs qualification criteria, then Interac provides a flat reimbursement fee per transaction rather than an Ad Valorem amount. This amount will be a credit.
SMS Notification Fee
Per Notification
This fee applies when Interac sends a notification via SMS for Send Money and Request Money transactions. A fee applies from Interac for each notification sent via SMS and when notification preferences are set to âSMS or Phoneâ or âSMS, or Phone, and emailâ.
High Volume Recipient
Per Transaction
This is an Interac fee for high volume recipients. The fee only applies if there are more than 2,000 transactions in the prior month for a single recipient and only for transaction number 2001 and beyond.
Monthly Maintenance and Support
Monthly
This is a monthly maintenance and support fee.
*
Fee also includes the recovery of 5% GST on the Interac portion of the fee.
Additionally, a detailed billing report is provided near mid-month. See Section 8.6, Interac e-Transfer Billing Report, for more information.
END
